critique: Strong adherence to the Swiss-editorial system: black/white palette, DM Mono for data, Inter for body, warm sand accent bar and divider, zero border-radius, left-anchored stat panel. The 9x stat at ~100px is bold and immediately readable. A few issues to address: (1) The stat-side background uses a subtle warm tint (#faf9f6) with a box-shadow that introduces a very faint gradient-like effect — the design system prohibits gradients, so this should be flattened to pure white or #faf9f6 without the shadow. (2) The warm-bar at top uses a `linear-gradient` to transparent, which technically violates the 'no gradients' rule. (3) The dot/constellation SVG decoration is a nice custom touch but the dots in the stat panel (left column) feel visually competitive with the large stat value — reduce opacity further or remove from left panel entirely. (4) 'Europe YoY Revenue Growth' as a unit label is slightly long for a mono caption beneath a giant number; trimming to 'YoY Revenue Growth · Europe' or similar would feel tighter. (5) The footer URL and brand lockup overlap slightly in z-space — add a clear separator or align them to the same baseline. Overall a competent, on-brand execution with minor polish needed.
visionCritique: The card closely mirrors Flywheel's brand aesthetic with the floating dot/bubble particle background, cream/off-white background, and clean sans-serif typography — all strong matches to the reference. The split-panel layout (large stat left, explanatory text right) is clear and functional. However, the left panel feels slightly underdesigned with too much empty vertical space above and below the '9x' figure. The teal/terracotta colored label text ('EUROPE YOY REVENUE GROWTH') introduces a brand color that doesn't appear in the reference screenshot and feels slightly off. The divider line under the headline on the right panel is a nice touch but very subtle. To improve: tighten the vertical centering of the stat, ensure accent colors are pulled from the verified brand palette (the reference uses primarily black/gray/white), and consider making the 'DATA POINT' label slightly more prominent or styled consistently with Flywheel's typographic voice.
